title: Myth Busting Genetic Research with MVP: MVP Surveys
summary: Fact: Survey information is foundational to all research at VA's Million Veteran Program (MVP). MVP combines this with Veterans' health records and blood sample to help researchers understand connections between genes and health, which can lead to discoveries that can improve care for Veterans. Learn more and join today at https://www.mvp.va.gov or call 866-441-6075.

title: Myth Busting Genetic Research with MVP: MVP is For Everyone
summary: Fact: VA’s Million Veteran Program (MVP), a genetic and health research initiative, is for all Veterans who served in the United States military. No matter your age, race, ethnicity, or service branch, you can join MVP. Learn more and join today at https://www.mvp.va.gov or call 866-441-6075.

title: Myth Busting Genetic Research with MVP: How MVP Uses Veteran Data
summary: Fact: Protecting the confidentiality, privacy, and security of research participants is a top priority for VA’s Million Veteran Program (MVP). MVP removes identifying details such as names, dates of birth, and SSNs; and uses advanced technology to secure enrolled Veterans’ information. Learn more and join today at https://www.mvp.va.gov or call 866-441-6075.

title: Department of the Navy Creates Portfolio Aqcuisition Executive-Marine Corps to Accelerate Modernization
summary: On Apr 10, the Marine Corps took a historic step in its modernization journey with the establishment of the Portfolio Acquisition Executive-Marine Corps (PAE-MC). This new organization replaces Program Executive Office Land Systems (PEO-LS) and incorporates programs from Marine Corps Systems Command (MCSC), marking a significant transformation in how the Marine Corps develops and delivers capabilities to its warfighters.

source: The Official United States Marine Corps Public Website News
title: Blount Island Command Team Supports First-Ever MPF Offload in Mindanao
summary: A Marine Corps technical assistance and advisory team deployed in March from Jacksonville, Florida, in support of a first-of-its-kind maritime prepositioning force offload in the Indo-Pacific region.

title: CIA Insider: Why the Iran Ceasefire is Extremely Dangerous
summary:
Retired CIA Operations Officer Sam Faddis breaks down the fragile new ceasefire with Iran and why the negotiated terms should worry every American.

In this hard-hitting episode, Sam reveals:
• Why the current Iran ceasefire is far more dangerous than it looks
• Iran’s true intentions driven by radical religious beliefs
• The hidden nuclear weapons strategy that changes everything
• How civilian intelligence and military operators actually work together
• The incredible story of American airmen recovered from a hostile environment

From fragile diplomatic deals to high-stakes nuclear brinkmanship and real-world rescue operations — this is unfiltered insight from a career CIA veteran who has seen it all.

Timestamps:
00:00 Ceasefire with Iran
11:00 Nuclear Weapons and Strategy
17:29 Recovery of Airmen

Guest: Retired CIA Operations Officer Sam Faddis
